name: Charlotte (Narrator)
_note: Default narrator voice (ElevenLabs preset 'Charlotte') used for all unattributed prose and chapter epigraphs. The narrator is canonically Kirsten Strand — but she is *read* in Charlotte's voice, intentionally distinct from Strand's in-scene voice (see characters.json → kirsten-strand). The audible split mirrors the book's premise: Strand-the-character speaks in chapters 23 and 24, while Strand-the-narrator-from-beyond addresses 'Next' in a different register entirely. This entry mirrors the default narrator voice configured in the Edge Function at functions/v1/narrate — keep them in sync if you change one.

name: Brisinger Crew
_note: Voice-only entry for the integrated/unified Bris crew speakers. Not displayed in the wiki.

name: Guardian
_note: Voice-only entry for Ivaldi/Plateau guardians (Order of Magicians enforcers). First speaks in chapter 13 — ritual questions at the chamber steps, and short directives. Fill in the ElevenLabs voice_id.

name: The Song
_note: Voice-only entry for the melodic 'beautiful voice' that emerges through Gary the CIX when the alien signal first translates into chant (Chapter 18 — 'Song that Silence Mothers'). A clear, resonant, low melodic voice — 'as if emerging from the ocean's depths.' Distinct from Gary's regular CIX voice; this is what Mairee sings *through* the CIX. Fill in an ElevenLabs voice tuned for singing/chant, ideally with multilingual_v2 stability low for melodic delivery.

name: Samantha (Sam)
_note: Voice-only entry for Samantha — a guardian officer working under Centurion Jax. Short curls; calls herself Sam in the field. First speaks in Chapter 18 ('Song that Silence Mothers'), reporting Zoë's complaint to Jax and then standing outside the nursery office while Jax assaults Zoë — the chapter's bystander arc. Inner-voice italicised thoughts ('Go in. Stop him.' / 'If you don't move now…') also tagged to this speaker.

name: Hull Whisper
_note: Voice-only entry for the subsonic whisper Dominic Beltrán transmits through the FNS Dáinn's grav-cycler resonance into the hull (Chapter 19 — 'Portamus Futurum'). Heard only when an ear is pressed against the bulkhead through a drinking glass. ElevenLabs voice should be tuned for soft, intimate, near-inaudible whisper delivery — low stability, low style, minimal projection. Distinct from Beltrán's regular voice. Reused for any subsequent covert hull-vibration whispers the resistance sends along the same channel.

name: Older Guardian
_note: Voice-only entry for the older, predatory perimeter guardian who intercepts Lysanne and Oliver at the industrial loading gate in Chapter 21 ('Full Reverse'). A canyon of cynical wrinkles, leering, quoting the Centurion's 'every able body is a colony asset' doctrine. Lower, rougher, slower delivery than the standard 'guardian' voice — the menace of long service, casual cruelty earned from rank. Distinct from the cryo-bay guardian (kept on the default 'guardian' voice).
